trails.name: Pyramid Track
trails.description: An access easement to the Ben Ohau Range, Darts Bush Stream and Flanagan Pass.

trails.name: Ahuriri Valley Track
trails.description: This track follows the Ahuriri River. Three huts on route provide several options.

trails.name: HIKUWAI LINK TRACK
trails.description: A popular, gently-graded link track that connects Gunn Road with the Outlet Track.

trails.name: HAAST PASS LOOKOUT TRACK
trails.description: A short climb through native forest to the bush-line, with a good view of Mount Brewster.

trails.name: LINDIS PEAK TRACK
trails.description: This farm track to the 1,226 m summit of Lindis Peak offers great panoramic views.

trails.name: PLANK CREEK to TUOHYS SADDLE Tk
BERWEN  TRACK
trails.description: This track crosses private farmland and links the East Manuherikia 4WD Track with the Hawkdun Range.

trails.name: MUTTON CREEK TRACK
trails.description: This track links the lower sections of the West and East Manuherikia 4WD Tracks.

trails.name: Greta Track
trails.description: Pass by fellfields (blocky scree) and short rock bluffs above Gretas Stream. This track is close to Lake Ōhau.

trails.name: TINWALD BURN RIDGE TRACK
trails.description: A steep 1,882 m hike from the valley floor to the Pisa Range Ridge.

trails.name: UPPER CLUTHA RIVER TRACK
trails.description: Walk or cycle along the banks of the Clutha River near Lake Wānaka.

trails.name: DEVILS CREEK TRACK
trails.description: This access track to the Bendigo Conservation Area provides good views of Lake Dunstan and the Pisa Range.

trails.name: OUTLET TRACK
trails.description: A picturesque riverside walk along the Clutha River/Mata-Au. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: BRIDLE TRACK
trails.description: This well-graded track follows a section of the old Bridle Track, the first link between Otago and Westland.

trails.name: CAMERON LOOKOUT WALK
trails.description: A short climb through the bush to a viewing platform overlooking Cameron Creek.

trails.name: Temple Viewpoint Track
trails.description: This short track through mountain beech forest provides good views into South Temple Valley.

trails.name: River Access @ BA Boundary easement TAT
AROUND BLUE LAKE TRACK
trails.description: This track has good views of Blue Lake. The St Bathans to Domain track offers an off-road route to the St Bathans Domain.

trails.name: MOU WAHO ISLAND - ARETHUSA POOL TK
SAWYER BURN TRACK
trails.description: The Sawyer Burn Track climbs through the forest to the bushline and has superb views of Lake Hāwea and the surrounding mountains.

trails.name: BLUE VALLEY TRACK
trails.description: The track to Camp Flat is easily accessible and an interesting day trip. You can continue on for another 13 km to the head of the valley.

trails.name: LITTLE CRIFFEL TRACK
trails.description: This track is in the Pisa Conservation Area which lies between Wānaka and Cromwell.

trails.name: Cardrona Cromwell Pk Tk(to Pisa CA bound
DINGLE BURN PENINSULA TRACK
trails.description: The Dingle Burn Peninsula Track is a pleasant walk to the Turihuka Conservation Area. It also provides access into Hāwea Conservation Park via the Dingle Burn and Hunter River Tracks.

trails.name: ISTHMUS PEAK TRACK
trails.description: Isthmus Peak Track is a steep and challenging but rewarding hike located between Lake Wānaka and Lake Hāwea, offering panoramic views of both lakes and the Southern Alps.

trails.name: GLEN DENE RIDGE TRACK
trails.description: Cross the undulating skyline ridge on Glen Dene Ridge Track.

trails.name: HOMESTEAD TRACK
trails.description: This short track that follows an old water race.

trails.name: LOCHARBURN TRACK
trails.description: Access the Locharburn Scenic Reserve and Pisa Conservation Area via a steep, direct hike.

trails.name: KIRTLE BURN TRACK
trails.description: Kirtle Burn Track starts at the Western side of the Pisa Conservation Area, accessing Kirtle Burn Hut, and giving access to Mt Pisa.

trails.name: BOUNDARY CREEK TRACK
trails.description: A pleasant climb up an old musterers' track above an upland river gorge.

trails.name: MINARET BURN TRACK
trails.description: This walk passes through native forest and bush, crossing burns (streams) and giving access to Lake Wānaka itself. This track is suitable for walking, mountain biking and horse riding.

trails.name: MELINA RIDGE TRACK
trails.description: This is a classic mountain bike ride located in lower Ahuriri valley.

trails.name: GRANDVIEW MOUNTAIN TRACK
trails.description: Trampers are well rewarded for their effort from the upper sections of the greater Grandview Range with inspiring views over Lakes Hāwea and Wānaka and mountains beyond.

trails.name: LUGGATE CREEK TRACK
trails.description: This short, easy amble leads from Luggate township down to the Clutha River.

trails.name: Flanagan Pass trail
trails.description: A link track from the Greta Stream Track which crosses Flanagan Pass and connects to the Pyramid Track at Darts Bush Stream. It is also possible to 4 wheel drive to Dorcy Airstrip and walk to the Pass from there.

trails.name: DRY CREEK TRACK
trails.description: Dry Creek Track provides great backcountry opportunities for short walks, tramping and mountain biking with access to the Dry Creek and Neinei i Kura Conservation areas.

trails.name: CLOUDY PEAK TRACK
trails.description: The Cloudy Peak Track from its 1526 m high point, provides fantastic views of the Upper Clutha plains and peaks of the Lindis and St Bathans ranges.

trails.name: NEWCASTLE TRACK
trails.description: Suitable for biking and walking this track follows the true left of the Clutha River from Hāwea River swing bridge to Kane Road.

trails.name: CLUDEN CK CONS AREA tk - bottom easement
CLUDEN CK CONS AREA tk - top easement
YOUNG CAR PARK TO MOUTH TRACK
Albert Burn Hut Track
trails.description: From the mouth of the Albert Burn follow the farmed river flats into the gorge and on to the Albert Burn Hut.

trails.name: Birch Creek access route
trails.description: This public access route travels along Birch Creek in the Ahuriri valley.

trails.name: Blue Lake walks
trails.description: Enjoy one of the walks around historic, man-made Blue Lake in St Bathans and view sluicings and gold mining remains.

trails.name: Blue Pools Track
trails.description: Nestled among mature beech and podocarp forest, these pools of deep, clear water flowing into the Makarora River offer a moment of tranquillity.

trails.name: Blue Valley Track
trails.description: The track to Camp Flat is easily accessible and an interesting day trip. You can continue on for another 13 km to the head of the valley.

trails.name: Young River Link Track
trails.description: An extension of the very popular Blue Pools Walk and connects with the Young River. It's  also an alternative, high river route to the stat of the Gillespie Pass Circuit.

trails.name: Butterfields Wetland Walk
trails.description: Situated near the Hāwea River Swing Bridge this small loop track around the Butterfields Wildlife Management Area is suitable for biking, running and walking.

trails.name: Cameron Valley Track
trails.description: Starting at the Cameron Creek car park enjoy a good day's tramp ending at the 4-bunk Cameron Hut.

trails.name: Canyon Creek Track
trails.description: Discover spectacular waterfalls and a rock bivvy in the Canyon Creek area.

trails.name: Craig Burn Track
trails.description: Read about the gold mining history of the area at the carpark then walk to a pleasant picnic spot.

trails.name: Deans Bank Track
trails.description: This loop walking and mountain biking track meanders along the upper slopes of the Clutha river and offers spectacular views of surrounding peaks and Otago landscapes.

trails.name: Dromedary Hill Track
trails.description: It is well worth the effort getting out the car and stretching your legs with a day hike up Dromedary Hill.

trails.name: East Ahuriri Track
trails.description: East Ahuriri Track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Ewe Range Track
trails.description: The Ewe Range Track provides walking access to the northern end of Oteake Conservation Park.

trails.name: Hideaway Hut Route
trails.description: This little gem of a hut is tucked away in Ahuriri Conservation Park.

trails.name: Kānuka Track
trails.description: This 10 km return loop offers views of Lake Dunstan and the Upper Clutha Valley as it weaves its way through regenerated kānuka shrublands and impressive rocky cliffs.

trails.name: Kidds Bush Nature Walk
trails.description: The Kidds Bush Nature Walk is an enjoyable loop track through a mountain beech forest.

trails.name: Lake Ōhau Track
trails.description: This track runs along the Lake Ōhau shoreline at its moraine end. This is a stunning location in both summer and winter with great views of Ben Ohau Peak and Lake Ōhau.

trails.name: Makarora Valley Track
trails.description: Experienced trampers can walk to the 4-bunk Makarora Hut, taking in the Stewart Falls along the way.

trails.name: Manuherikia River tracks
trails.description: These well-formed tracks lead into Oteake Conservation Park.

trails.name: Melina Ridge Track
trails.description: This is a classic mountain bike ride located in lower Ahuriri valley.

trails.name: Minaret Burn Track
trails.description: This walk passes through native forest and bush, crossing burns (streams) and giving access to Lake Wānaka itself. This track is suitable for walking, mountain biking and horse riding.

trails.name: Brewster Track
trails.description: The track starts on State Highway 6, crosses the unbridged Haast River, and climbs steadily through beech forest to the bush line. The track ends at Brewster Hut.

trails.name: Mount Iron Track
trails.description: This very popular loop walk offers excellent 360-degree views from the summit.

trails.name: Mount Shrimpton Track
trails.description: A good, steep bush walk to the uplands of the McKerrow Range. Great views of the Makarora Valley below and the alps beyond.

trails.name: Pavilion Peak Track
trails.description: This track traverses a range in the Lindis Conservation Area.

trails.name: Quartz Reef Point Walk
trails.description: Take a short walk to Quartz Reef Point, the best preserved example of herring bone tailings in the country.

trails.name: Skyline Track
trails.description: The Skyline Track is a challenging tramping track in the Wānaka area.

trails.name: Temple valley tracks
trails.description: These short tramping tracks travel through mountain beech forest.

trails.name: Top Dingle Hut Track
trails.description: A track climbs from Ahuriri valley over Dingleburn Pass and descends to Top Dingle Hut in Dingle Burn valley.

trails.name: Wairepo Kettleholes Track
trails.description: Wairepo Kettleholes provide a feeding area for both native and introduced birds.

trails.name: Wether Range Tracks
trails.description: The Wether Range tracks are found south of Omarama.

trails.name: Wills Valley Track
trails.description: This track is for experienced trampers and starts at the Gates of Haast Bridge. Like most of the smaller valleys in this area, the Wills’ lower reaches are gorged, and then open out to flats further up.

trails.name: Hunter Valley tracks
trails.description: The Hunter River Valley is part of the Hāwea Conservation Park and nestled between the Young and Huxley ranges. Its open grasslands, braided river system and deep canyon gorges offer remote seekers a beautiful but challenging backcountry experience.

trails.name: Awa Nohoaka Conservation Area access tracks
trails.description: Short tussock and pasture grasslands contrast with grey shrublands, dry shrublands and kanuka shrublands make up this unique conservation area.

trails.name: Tarnbrae Track A2O
trails.description: Traverse glacial terraces through Freehold Creek beech forest. Part of the Alps 2 Ocean (A2O) Cycle Trail, the high point on this track and offers fabulous views of Lake Ohau and the surrounding country.

trails.name: Sawyers and Parsons Creek to Freehold Creek Track
trails.description: Climb up past mountain beech and native mistletoes to an ancient glacial moraine terrace in the Lake Ōhau area. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Makarora Bush Walk
trails.description: A short loop walk through native forest.

trails.name: Hāwea River Track
trails.description: Suitable for all ages and experience levels, this gentle riverside track links Albert Town to Hāwea. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Aurora Track
trails.description: A short loop through kānuka shrubland, featuring the remains of old stone huts.

trails.name: Roaring Meg Pack Track
trails.description: The Roaring Meg Pack Track can be completed in a day or as an overnight tramp staying at Meg Hut. It goes up and over the Pisa Range from Cardrona valley to Kawarau Gorge.

trails.name: Cardrona-Cromwell Pack Track
trails.description: This overnight tramp goes up and over the Pisa Range from Cardrona Valley to Lowburn.

trails.name: Blue Pools Track
trails.description: Nestled among mature beech and podocarp forest, these pools of deep, clear water flowing into the Makarora River offer a moment of tranquillity.

trails.name: Roys Peak Track
trails.description: Take in breathtaking views over Lake Wānaka, Mount Aspiring/Tititea and surrounding peaks after a steep climb through alpine meadows and tussock grasslands to the summit. This is a popular track and one of our best day hikes.

trails.name: Swann Road to Deep Creek Hut route
